title: Space Force awards Slingshot $69 million for AI-enabled training technology
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Slingshot a $69 million contract to develop AI-enabled training technology for simulated satellite-defense scenarios over 4.5 years. This initiative aims to enhance military readiness in space operations.

title: Saltzman’s farewell warning: Prepare for war in space to preserve peace
summary: Gen. Chance Saltzman, Space Force chief, warned in his farewell speech about the need to prepare for potential war in space to maintain peace. He emphasized combat credibility, stronger alliances, and realistic procurement reforms.

title: Commercial Space Federation Launches State and Local Council to Align States on National Space Capacity and Strengthen the U.S. Industrial Base
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) launched a State and Local Council to align U.S. states on national space capacity and bolster the industrial base. This initiative aims to strengthen the commercial space sector's collaboration with state and local governments.

title: Startup working with University of Texas focuses on trapping small debris
summary: SOAR, a Florida startup, partnered with the University of Texas at El Paso to develop a passive system for trapping small space debris. The collaboration aims to address the growing issue of orbital debris.

title: Icarus Robotics taps KULR to provide batteries for Space Station robots
summary: Icarus Robotics, a New York-based startup, has selected KULR Technology Group to supply batteries for Joy, a free-flying robot platform intended for space station missions. This collaboration highlights advancements in space robotics and battery technology.

title: Laying the groundwork for the first human mission to Mars
summary: The article discusses the complexities of planning the first human mission to Mars, highlighting the numerous challenges involved in safely sending and returning astronauts. It emphasizes the foundational work required for such an ambitious endeavor.

title: Satellite investment sets annual record halfway through 2026
summary: Investment in satellite companies hit $8.1 billion in the first half of 2026, already exceeding the total annual investment recorded in previous years. This milestone was highlighted by early-stage investor Space Capital.

title: Japan seeks to ramp up launch activity
summary: Japan aims to significantly increase its launch frequency despite ongoing challenges with existing and new launch vehicles. The government is pushing for expanded launch activities to boost the country's space industry.

title: Isaacman attends Soyuz launch of ISS crew
summary: Jared Isaacman, NASA Administrator, attended the Soyuz launch that sent a new crew to the International Space Station on July 14. This marks a significant moment in international space collaboration.

title: Space acquisition and NRO picks face SASC
summary: Erich Hernandez-Baquero and Roger Mason, nominated for key space roles, testified before the Senate Armed Services Committee. Their nominations for Air Force space acquisition and NRO leadership were reviewed.
company: U.S. Air Force, National Reconnaissance Office

title: 'Full steam ahead': NASA ramps up prep for Artemis III astronaut launch in 2027
summary: NASA is accelerating preparations for the Artemis III mission, targeting a 2027 launch with crewed lunar ambitions. Hardware assembly and launch simulations are underway as the agency advances its moon program.

title: Watch Archimedes burn! Rocket Lab fires up engine for its powerful next-gen Neutron launcher (video)
summary: Rocket Lab conducted a full-duration static fire test of the engine for the second stage of its upcoming Neutron launch vehicle. This marks a significant milestone in the development of Neutron, Rocket Lab's next-generation rocket.

title: Meteorite that crashed through New Jersey house could hold the clues to life's origins
summary: A meteorite that crashed through a New Jersey house may provide insights into the origins of life. The pristine condition of the CM1/2 meteorite is attributed to the homeowner's quick action.

title: Raspberry sugar spotted in interstellar space, a sweet discovery that could reveal clues about life's origins
summary: Scientists discovered raspberry sugar (methyl formate) in interstellar space, suggesting sugars can form naturally in space. This discovery may provide insights into the origins of life.

title: The 10 best places in the UK to see the partial solar eclipse on Aug. 12
summary: The UK will experience its deepest partial solar eclipse in decades on August 12, coinciding with the peak of the Perseid meteor shower. The news highlights the best locations in the UK to view the eclipse.

title: Glaciers spotted flowing across the Himalayas from space | Space photo of the day for July 15, 2026
summary: Satellite imagery captured glaciers flowing across the Himalayas. The space photo highlights the movement of glacial ice downhill.

title: 'Found you!' Astronomers spot faintest exoplanet ever seen from Earth after a decade of hide-and-seek
summary: Astronomers have discovered the faintest exoplanet ever observed from Earth, located around the star Beta Pictoris, after a decade-long search. The planet was found using advanced observational techniques, ending a prolonged game of cosmic hide-and-seek.

title: Every SpaceX Starlink satellite has to dodge a collision almost weekly, and experts fear the worst
summary: SpaceX's Starlink satellites performed over 355,000 collision avoidance maneuvers in the past year, with each satellite dodging objects nearly weekly. Experts warn this high frequency of evasive actions could lead to future risks.

title: What time is SpaceX's Starship Flight 13 launch on July 16? (Full mission timeline)
summary: SpaceX is scheduled to launch its 13th Starship test flight on July 16, carrying the first V3 Starlink satellites. The launch marks a significant step in SpaceX's Starship and Starlink programs.

title: NASA Uses Subscale Aircraft to Accelerate Flight Innovation
summary: NASA is accelerating flight innovation by testing new aerospace concepts using small, remotely piloted and autonomous aircraft at the Dale Reed Subscale Flight Research Laboratory in Edwards, California. This approach helps mature innovative ideas and reduce risk in a cost-effective manner.

title: NASA Study of Pristine Meteorite Adds to Story of Ancient Asteroids
summary: A meteorite recovered immediately after its fall in July 2024 is providing NASA scientists with new insights into ancient water, chemical evolution of primitive asteroids, and potential ingredients for life in the early solar system.

title: How NASA’s Artemis III Lander Test Will Pave Way for Moon Landings
summary: NASA will conduct the Artemis III lander test mission in 2027 to practice rendezvous and docking operations before astronauts land on the Moon in 2028. This mission will provide critical data for future uncrewed demonstrations at the Moon.

title: Anil Menon Launches to Space Station
summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon launched to the International Space Station aboard a Soyuz rocket as part of the Soyuz MS-29 mission. The launch was observed by his family, including astronaut candidate Anna Menon.

title: Curiosity Finds Evidence of an Ancient Sandstorm
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover discovered evidence of an ancient, intense sandstorm on Mars that created layered sedimentary rocks. The storm, which lasted hours, occurred billions of years ago and was documented in an image taken on December 12, 2024.

title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Provides Sweeping View of Broom Point
summary: NASA's Perseverance Rover captured a sweeping view of the rim of Jezero Crater on Mars using its Mastcam-Z instrument. The image shows bright-colored rocks exposed across the slope, taken on May 15, 2025.

title: Perseverance’s Trip to ‘Broom Point’
summary: NASA's Perseverance Mars rover reached a location called 'Broom Point member,' a layered bedrock formation over 3.9 billion years old. The rover landed in Jezero Crater in February 2021 and is exploring the crater's western delta.

title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Reads Record of Ancient Mars Impacts
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover discovered a 75-meter-thick stack of ancient rock on Mars' Jezero Crater rim, formed by repeated asteroid impacts over 3.9 billion years ago. This finding provides insights into the planet's early history and impact events.

title: Volunteer Measures Record Louisiana Rainfall
summary: Volunteers are measuring record rainfall in Louisiana to contribute data for scientific research and weather forecasting. This effort supports a national community of precipitation reporters.

title: NASA’s Webb Discovers Hidden Planet in Famous Star System
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope discovered a hidden giant exoplanet in the Beta Pictoris star system, which was already known to host two other giant planets. This finding adds to the understanding of planetary formation in a well-studied young star system.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for the first land recovery of a rocket booster.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This marks another mission to sustain operations and research aboard the orbital laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS) this week, while SpaceX's Starship is scheduled for its 13th flight test. Seven launches are planned globally.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key milestone ahead of its upcoming launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: Booster 20, a SpaceX rocket, completed a record-duration static fire test with 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in SpaceX's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being built for Stoke Space and Relativity Space, marking a continued revitalization of the region's space industry infrastructure.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to be the world's first commercial space station.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is advancing preparations for a Starship launch from Cape Canaveral this year, with contractors actively involved. The company is pushing to meet a potential launch timeline.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The news source is a B2B website focused on the technology, engineering, and construction sectors, covering business-related content. No specific space industry news was provided in the input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025. These events aim to inform industry about planned activities and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential impact on future missions. This innovation could significantly enhance robotic operations in space.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust or resilient technology, likely in the space industry. However, the provided text lacks specific details about the context or company involved.

title: Exclusive: Loft Orbital Scales Offering with Apex Bus Purchase
summary: Loft Orbital acquired new satellite bus inventory to support larger, power-intensive customer payloads. This move expands their offerings in the space industry.

title: NatSec Space Policy Nominees Face Questions from Senators
summary: Pentagon nominees for space policy leadership faced scrutiny from senators regarding modern acquisition reforms. The nominee for space acquisition highlighted that legacy space programs were not designed for current reforms.

title: Payload Field Guide: NSSL
summary: The U.S. Space Force expanded its National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program by adding two new launch providers on July 8, increasing the total number of companies in the program to seven.

title: Spinifex Space Launches With Australian Test Ranges
summary: Spinifex Space has launched operations to offer end-to-end suborbital launch campaigns and private range access in Australia. The company will also provide test and evaluation infrastructure for space missions.

title: Lunar Outpost Joins ESA’s Moonraker Mission in 2030
summary: Lunar Outpost Europe will contribute thermal management solutions to ESA's Moonraker mission, scheduled for 2030. The collaboration aims to help the spacecraft endure extreme temperature variations in lunar orbit.

title: Reditus Finishes ENOS Reentry Vehicle Ahead of First Flight
summary: Reditus has completed the development of its first reentry spacecraft, ENOS, ahead of its scheduled debut flight this fall. This marks a significant milestone for the company in advancing its space capabilities.

title: Reflect Orbital Cleared to Fly In-Orbit Mirror by FCC
summary: Reflect Orbital has received FCC clearance to proceed with its in-orbit mirror mission. The FCC stated that concerns about environmental and astronomical impacts fall outside its jurisdiction.

title: Pulse Space Lands $40M Space Force Contract to Develop Laser Tech
summary: Pulse Space, a Washington-based startup, secured a $40 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop laser technology for satellite power beaming. The company focuses on in-space laser systems capable of transferring energy between satellites.

title: US Government Studying Offshore Launch Opportunities
summary: The U.S. government is exploring offshore launch opportunities by studying the feasibility of building or repurposing infrastructure on the Outer Continental Shelf for space launches and reentries. The Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (BOEM) issued a Request for Information (RFI) to gather relevant data.

title: NATO Allies Plan to Stitch Together Sovereign Space Fleets
summary: Eight NATO allies announced plans to integrate their national defense satellites into a unified network. This initiative aims to enhance collective security and space-based defense capabilities.

title: FCC to Consider Allowing Wi-Fi, Bluetooth Devices for D2D Service
summary: The FCC is considering allowing unlicensed wireless devices like Wi-Fi and Bluetooth to communicate directly with satellites for direct-to-device (D2D) service. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr proposed a new proceeding to explore this possibility.

title: South Africa’s Top ISP Herotel to Distribute Amazon Leo
summary: South Africa's leading ISP Herotel will distribute Amazon's Leo satellite connectivity to residential customers. The deal marks Herotel's expansion into satellite internet distribution.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense COO Kevin Bidlack Talks Growth Post-IPO in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense's COO Kevin Bidlack discusses the company's growth following its recent IPO in a Thursday Morning Conversation. The company, a diverse space tech firm, is capitalizing on favorable market conditions post-IPO.

title: Slingshot Aerospace Wins Space Force Contract to Expand AI Mission Rehearsal
summary: Slingshot Aerospace secured a nearly $70 million contract with the U.S. Space Force, marking the largest deal in its history. The agreement focuses on expanding AI-driven mission rehearsal capabilities.

title: SDA Set to Resume Launches With Second Plane of York Transport Layer Satellites
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) is set to resume launches with the second plane of 21 York Space Systems data transport satellites after a nine-month pause. The launch marks a significant step in the York Transport Layer satellite program.

title: Honeywell to Lead ESA Quantum Space Magnetometer Contract
summary: Honeywell Aerospace will lead a consortium to develop a compact quantum space magnetometer for ESA, aiming to advance Earth's magnetic field research. The project involves partners Quantum Brilliance and Jagiellonian University.

title: NRL Awards Parsons $245M Satellite Ground Systems Contract for Tracking Facility
summary: The U.S. Naval Research Laboratory awarded Parsons Corporation a $245 million contract to support satellite ground systems software and operations at a tracking facility. The contract continues Parsons' work in this area.

title: Frontier to Adopt Starlink With New Customer Portal From SpaceX
summary: Frontier Airlines will adopt SpaceX's Starlink in-flight connectivity for over 1,000 aircraft, following a new deal announced with SpaceX. This marks a significant expansion of Starlink's aviation services.

title: Iridium’s PNT Chip is Now Commercially Available
summary: Iridium has announced the commercial availability of its positioning, navigation, and timing (PNT) chip, with over 150 organizations expressing interest. The chip was first introduced in October.

title: L3Harris and Sierra Space Receive $1.75B for SDA Tranche 3, Tracking Layer
summary: L3Harris and Sierra Space secured $1.75 billion in contracts for the Space Development Agency's Tranche 3 Tracking Layer satellites. L3Harris received $955 million and Sierra Space $798 million under rapid prototyping agreements.
